title: Roegneria yezoensis
taxonomy: Roegneria yezoensis is a species of grass in the subfamily Pooideae of the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Triticeae. The genus Roegneria is part of the subfamily Pooideae, which also includes many other grass species. Roegneria yezoensis is native to East Asia, specifically in Japan and Korea. Roegneria yezoensis is known to be a cold-hardy and drought-tolerant species.

intro: Roegneria yezoensis, also known as Japanese wild barley or Yezo wild barley, is a perennial grass species native to Japan. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions such as arthritis, hypertension, and autoimmune diseases. The bioactive constituents of Roegneria yezoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Roegneria yezoensis is often prepared as an extract or decoction, and is also used in the form of tea, capsules, or tablets to support overall health and well-being.
